title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I am so excited to see my family and we are going to be making so many videos here of real-life English situations so make sure to subscribe to our channel and check out the pinned comment below.

title: will / shall
examples: We failed to check in online last night so we will head to the checkout counter first before going through security. We've got three pieces of luggage. I hope they're not overweight.

I was so nervous when I put my bag on the scale but none of my suitcases were overweight so I'm very happy. Luckily I made it through security without any issues. I have a lot of time to kill before my boarding time. So my husband said he'll take me shopping at the duty free shops.
Let's go Gucci. I checked my boarding pass again and we will board our plane at Gate 247. So after shopping we will go to our gate. That's so creepy bro.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: We didn't spend all of our money at the duty free shops so now we are at the currency exchange so we can change our Korean one into US dollars. Don't you say oh Hello. Can I get USD? Yes please. I'm kissing my money. Goodbye.
You can have a look please.

We have finally landed in Detroit, Michigan. It was a long flight but it was a smooth flight so I can't really complain. Cab and crew was very friendly. We had good in flight entertainment and there wasn't much turbulence so I'm glad we got here safely.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: Let's go find our airline. I usually check the departure board to see where my airlines check-in counter is located. Today I am flying with Delta which is located at Gate 8. Let's go check my luggage. Where are you going? Uh, Detroit. Are you at 41st with you? No. I just finished checking in.
After going through immigration I grabbed our suitcases from baggage claim. They were located on carousel number two and now we are heading to grab a shuttle bus to go rent a car and get out of here.
